response.justification: While this candidate demonstrates exceptional software engineering fundamentals, comprehensive full-stack capabilities, and strong software craftsmanship practices, they lack the core Java/Kotlin and JVM expertise required for an enterprise IDE role. Their career trajectory emphasizes web/full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, React) rather than enterprise Java frameworks or JVM tooling. Limited IDE experience exists (LabWindows/CVI in C/C++), but doesn't transfer to the enterprise Java ecosystem.
strengths: Proven expertise in complex systems design and large-scale codebases (microservices handling millions of requests/sec, event-driven platforms)
Excellent software craftsmanship practices with demonstrated TDD, clean code, and mentorship through workshops and courses
Relevant IDE internals experience from LabWindows/CVI work at National Instruments, showing understanding of IDE component architecture
concerns: Minimal substantive Java or Kotlin experience; Java listed in skills but career history shows almost no Java project details despite being a required core competency
Complete absence of Spring, Hibernate, Quarkus, or other enterprise Java framework experience essential for the role

Career trajectory clearly moving toward web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, Ember.js, React) rather than toward enterprise JVM tooling—recent staff role emphasizes full-stack and AI infrastructure, not Java/IDE work

key_factors.factor: Software craftsmanship and systems design capability
